title: If I Were You – Concept Mastery Helper
author: Douglas James

concepts.lesson: Gerrard remains calm and uses his intelligence when the intruder threatens him with a gun. His quick thinking helps him control the situation.
concepts.real_example: In dangerous situations, staying calm and thinking quickly can help people make the right decisions.

concepts.lesson: The play contrasts Gerrard’s intelligence and confidence with the intruder’s violence and desperation.
concepts.real_example: Stories often highlight differences between characters to show good and bad qualities clearly.
concepts.source_section: Dialogue between Gerrard and the intruder

concepts.lesson: The entire story develops through dialogue between the two characters. Their conversation reveals their personalities and intentions.
concepts.real_example: In plays, dialogue is the main method used to move the story forward.

concepts.lesson: Gerrard often uses ironic statements to confuse the intruder and maintain control of the conversation.
concepts.real_example: People sometimes use irony to hide fear or to make a situation less tense.

concepts.lesson: The central conflict arises when the intruder enters Gerrard’s house and plans to kill him to assume his identity.
concepts.real_example: Conflict in stories creates tension and keeps readers interested.

concepts.lesson: Gerrard defeats the intruder not with physical strength but with clever reasoning and quick planning.
concepts.real_example: Many real-life problems can be solved through intelligence rather than force.

concepts.lesson: The intruder believes taking Gerrard’s identity will help him escape the police, showing how criminals may try to hide behind false identities.
concepts.real_example: Identity theft is a serious crime where people pretend to be someone else to escape consequences.

concepts.lesson: Gerrard manipulates the intruder psychologically by inventing a story about himself being a criminal, which makes the intruder hesitate.
concepts.real_example: People sometimes use clever communication to influence others’ decisions.

concepts.lesson: In the end, Gerrard traps the intruder and calls the police, showing that justice ultimately prevails over crime.
concepts.real_example: Legal systems exist to punish criminals and protect society.
